div.p: Encoding Flow: Assembly → REX (if needed) → Opcode → ModR/M (if operands) → Immediate (if present)
REX.W (0x48) enables 64-bit operands. Opcode C7 /0 is mov r/m64, imm32. ModR/M C0 = 11 000 000 (reg-to-reg, rax). Immediate 0x0000002A = 42 (little-endian).
REX.WB (0x49): W=64-bit, B=extended r/m. r8 requires REX.B bit set (r/m becomes r8). Opcode 89: mov r/m64, r64 (src in reg). ModR/M F8 = 11 111 000 (rdi→r8).
REX.W (0x48) for 64-bit operands. Opcode 01: add r/m64, r64 (src in reg). ModR/M F0 = 11 110 000: mod=11 (register), reg=110 (rsi), r/m=000 (rax). Result: rax = rax + rsi.
Opcode 6B: three-operand imul with sign-extended imm8. ModR/M C2 = 11 000 010: reg=000 (destination: rax), r/m=010 (source: rdx). Immediate 0x03 = 3 (sign-extended to 64 bits). Result: rax = rdx * 3.
Single-byte instruction: C3. Pops return address from stack and jumps to it. No REX prefix needed—no operands to encode. Used for function epilogue.
48 C7 C0 2A 00 00 00 49 89 F8 48 01 F0 48 6B C2 03 C3 |mov rax,42| |mov r8 |add rax| |imul__| |ret|
C7 = mov r/m, imm 89 = mov r/m, r 01 = add r/m, r 6B = imul r, r/m, imm8 C3 = ret
